activity.thought: I'm genuinely full right now—my body's telling me that clearly—and this good feeling is too valuable to waste. Exercise will use this energy productively, keep me healthy without spending money I don't have, and maybe clear my head about the rent stress that's lurking underneath. I can eat later when I actually need it.

I'm already full, so eating more right now won't actually help—my body doesn't need it. But I'm sharp and hopeful, and I should use this good headspace productively. Work would pay the bills that are crushing me, but the office isn't open yet. Exercise at the park sounds perfect: burn some energy, ride this momentum, and stay healthy without wasting money or time.

I'm in a really good headspace right now—sharp and hopeful—and my energy is solid. My stomach isn't screaming at me yet, but I did plan to eat, and skipping meals when I feel this good is usually a mistake. Plus I've got rent breathing down my neck at ⊙30 a day, so I should stick to the plan and fuel up before I lose this momentum.

blocks.label: Morning
blocks.note: I need fuel before a long day, and rent won't pay itself.

blocks.label: Afternoon
blocks.note: After sitting all day I need to move, and the park is free.

blocks.label: Evening
blocks.note: Long day, I'm hungry, and I should wind down before bed.
